rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxyselenocyclization of 1-allylnaphthalen-2-ols and allylphenols with diaryl diselenides to give selenylated dihydrofurans.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a selenium-centered radical cation/PhSe radical; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic/naphthyl cation after anodic oxidation of D (E)

electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh gives a Se-centered radical cation that fragments into PhSe• (Pathway I) or PhSe+ (Pathway II); the radical adds to the allyl alkene to form a C-radical that is further oxidized at the anode to a cation, which is trapped by the pendant naphthol OH (Pathway I); alternatively the PhSe+ forms a seleniranium that is opened by the phenol; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3.0 equiv TEMPO completely inhibits the reaction, supporting a radical pathway (Pathway I).

probe_reagents.name: PhSeBr (with electrolyte, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Affords 3a in 86% yield without electricity, supporting a PhSe+/seleniranium pathway (Pathway II).

probe_reagents.name: PhSeBr (no current, no nBu4NClO4)
probe_reagents.observation: Still gives 3a in 45%, consistent with electrophilic Se+ intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: Ar atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Under Ar with standard conditions, 3a obtained in 45% (vs 64% in air), indicating air/O2 plays a role but reaction still proceeds.

notes: Authors propose two parallel pathways: Pathway I (favored, radical) via anodic generation of PhSe•, alkene addition, second anodic oxidation to cation E, then intramolecular O-attack and deprotonation; Pathway II (cannot be ruled out) via PhSe+ cation forming a seleniranium F opened by the phenol. CV showed Epa = 1.63 V for 2a and 1.77 V for 1a (vs NHE), indicating diselenide is oxidized first. Gram-scale (6 mmol) gives 64% of 3a.
